title: Agent Skill Interchange v0.1 — .skillpack manifest
description: A portable envelope for moving a COLLECTION of skills between agent systems (a playground, an IDE, a registry, another vendor). SkillSpec (skill.schema.json) describes one SKILL.md; this describes the manifest that carries many of them together with provenance, integrity, and routing metadata. A .skillpack is a directory or archive containing this manifest as `skillpack.json` plus the referenced SKILL.md files.

spec.description: Format discriminator. Consumers MUST reject manifests whose spec they do not implement.

version.description: SemVer of the pack as a whole.

license.description: SPDX identifier for the pack; individual skills MAY override.

generator.description: Tool + version that produced the pack, e.g. 'pm-skills-export/1.2.0'.

skills.description: The skills carried by this pack. Each entry is SkillSpec frontmatter plus a pointer to the body and an integrity hash.

name.description: Unique within the pack; equals the skill's directory/file stem.

description.description: SkillSpec routing surface (what it does; 'Use when…'; 'Produces…').

path.description: Relative path within the pack to the skill's SKILL.md (or provider-native file).

format.description: Body format at `path`. 'skillmd' = SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter.

sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the file at `path`. Consumers SHOULD verify and MUST reject on mismatch when `integrity` is 'required'.

bundle.description: Optional grouping/plugin the skill belongs to.

provenance.description: Where this skill came from, for attribution and update.

metadata.description: Open extension. Consumers MUST ignore unknown keys.

integrity.description: How consumers MUST treat `sha256`. 'required' = reject the pack on any missing/mismatched hash.

compatibility.description: Optional hints about what runtimes this pack targets. Purely advisory — packs are provider-neutral by design.

minSkillspec.description: Minimum SkillSpec version the SKILL.md bodies conform to.

signatures.description: Optional detached signatures over the canonicalised manifest, for provenance beyond per-file hashes.
